C[ruster: hello, have a good game!
C[ruster: hello, have a good game!
C[Thomas: Yes, it is bold. Have a nice game.
C[Misawa: Amethel leaves for Minorca in one week. I follow her one week later!
Misawa: I’m headed to Minorca on Sunday! Can’t wait!
Thomas: I guess Minorca is 1,000 miles away from here. Currently in many parts of Europe we have the blackberry winter after real summer days. Hopefully Minorca is far away from us.

C[Misawa: Just got here last night. Took almost 24 hours of travel! But it is beautiful. Just had a nice European breakfast. Amethel has already been here for a week. She’s going to show me around for a couple of days. Then off to Barcelona
C[Thomas: Enjoy. It is said Menorca is famous for its cheese, provided you like products from cow´s milk.
Thomas: Maybe you are already more familiar with the Iberian culture than me.
C[Misawa: We have had some very nice cheese and sausage. Very peaceful here. Love it! We'll be putting some pictures up on Facebook.
C[Misawa: Arrived in Barcelona this morning. Quick stop. Leave the day after tomorrow for Morocco.
C[Misawa: Valencia tonight. Barcelona tomorrow. Then San Diego. Almost time to be home.
C[Thomas: Sounds like rush hour. I am happy that you both returned from the medieval trip :-)

C[Misawa: Rolling into San Diego now. Our flight took us to LA. Hired a Tesla to drive us back to San Diego. I’m writing you from that car now. Home in about 30 minutes! It’s about 1 AM here. Not sure what time my body thinks it is. Awesome trip!

Thomas: Perfect, you made it. I never experienced a restful slumber during a long distance flight. It took me at least the next night to get all chromosomes in line again. Get well soon and thx for sharing this awesome trip.
C[Misawa: Thank you. Yesterday was a weird jet lag day. Today...I just don't want to get back to work - but I'm trying. It was an awesome trip indeed. Still many photos to process.
C[Misawa: Looks like this one is done. Up for another?
C[Thomas: Many thanks for the game. I´m up for another :-)
Thomas: For the next game I again offer that you take black. Hopefully the invitation addresses this adequately. If not, decline.
Misawa: Looks good. Thank you for this game. See you on the next board!
C[Thomas: Welcome back. Have a nice game.
C[Thomas: Happy Easter for you and your family!
C[Misawa: Thank you and same to yours! Do you have plans with your daughters?
Thomas: Interestingly, they were both busy. So, enough time for short trips in twos.
C[Misawa: How old are they now? I’ve lost track.
C[Thomas: This year they we will turn 26 and 24 yrs. The sum may ring :-)
Misawa: Wow! That's amazing. How long have we been playing go together? It's been a long time!
C[Thomas: It is really amazing. You were 34 yrs., when we started our first game on 09-Sep-2003 on ""Little Golem"". In autumn 16 Years !

C[Misawa: Wow! That's nuts. So almost 16 years. I haven't been to Little Golem in years. I forgot that we started playing there. How funny. Can you still sign in and see how many games we played there?
C[Thomas: I have only my own records (excel file): 12 on LG, 15 on DGS (1 on Dave Peck's Go). 2007 we moved to OGS.
C[Misawa: 56 games! (If my math is right). Wow!

C[Thomas: 56 games is correct. I guess we share the joy in stats: Today I send our 11.295th move. That can be translated in 3.3 moves per day on average, every day in close to 16 years. That´s my wow! :-)
C[Misawa: Wow! That's wild! The things this game makes you do!
Misawa: By the way, have you seen the movies ""The Surrounding Game"" and ""AlphaGo""?
Misawa: Still can't get over it - 11,296 moves!
C[Thomas: Assumed, one move has taken 5 sec., all moves have been made in some more than 15 hours. That sounds like nothing in 16 years. In fact, I consider some moves over days back and forth.
C[Thomas: I haven´t seen both movies. Saw a trailer of the first one.
Misawa: Great stars. Just amazing. The guys who play live simultaneous games play almost that fast.
Misawa: Definitely check the movies out. Both are very good.
C[Thomas: Not sure what I actually planned with O1. Must have been the temps around 90F.
C[Misawa: LOL, that will do it. I try to play when I’m tired. Not a good idea.
C[Misawa: Sounds like you guys have had quite the heat wave?

C[Thomas: Wave is correct. 90F come and go.  This week we enjoy only 60F. The main problem is that it does not rain. In past a steady rainy weather, now nearly nothing. Still not a drought as last year, but it is still early.

C[Misawa: That's hot for your area. We aren't supposed to get much warmer than 85-90 degrees. Last year we had a whole week above 95. Maybe longer. It reached 112. Nasty nasty nasty. That was in June or July. It didn't rain until October or so. We've had light rain all the way into June this year. It will likely not rain again until October. It's very weird to go so long without rain.

C[Misawa: We just got home from a small tour of the southern and central California. We went to LA, Morro Bay, Sequoia National Park, and Yosemite National Park. It was a LOT of driving. California is just huge. But it was so beautiful. Keep an eye on our Facebook pages. Images to come!
C[Thomas: Many thanks for this invitation. When Germans think of their first trip to the US, the South-West is the answer.
C[Misawa: I can see why. It's another world.
C[Thomas: It was the highway 1 from SF down to LA, via Monterey, Big Sur, later on 101. California dreamin from Beach Boys. Mooro Bay must be famous for locals or I missed a hot spot :-)
Thomas: In fact, when Germans think of the South West , they long for the Monument Valley in Utah or Grand Canyon and the Sierra Nevada is something to pass.
C[Misawa: Interesting. We have really enjoyed being in this amazing state. We continue to be amazed by it. Utah is definitely on the list.

C[Thomas: In the 80s I travelled with a friend from Death Valley to SF by car, but I can´t recall the route. It was not Sequoia or Yosemite area and we did not pass Huntington Lake which you might have visited. Maybe, somewhere south of Reno. These amazing landscapes form your memories for the whole life.
Misawa: Very true. We feel so lucky that these places are within easy (easier) reach. Do you have any travel plans coming up? Amethel leaves for the Faroe Islands within a month. Sadly I can't go.
C[Thomas: No real plans.  Instead we invest(ed) in the refurbishment of our house. In fact, tomorrow we get our new kitchen.
Misawa: How exciting! Send me pictures!

C[Thomas: A vexing situation: An uncommon electric cable in the wall impeded one of four boreholes for the kitchen hood. The installation had to be stopped. I understood that a paneled wooden back wall may be the solution. We will have to discuss this with the kitchen studio.
C[Misawa: Sorry I'm slow to respond. Is it a cable that can't be moved? Most cables aren't too big a deal to move, unless they are heavy voltage.

C[Thomas: Seemingly it is the cable with heavy voltage, which comes up from the ""baking oven"". The new hood needed a bigger hole in the wall.  During the core drilling the cable and several others were torn to pieces.  All could be fixed, some moved. A friend is an electrician, but he could not offer any move. Tomorrow we will discuss it with the kitchen vendor.
C[Misawa: What a pain. Are your walls masonry or concrete? Or wood frame?
C[Thomas: With the new hole I learned that it is masonry.

Thomas: The appointment today offered a new option. We turned down a paneled wooden back wall along the whole backsplash. Now a 16 mm wooden board comes between hood and wall. It should work. The wooden board has to same color as the kitchen cabinets.
C[Misawa: Ok. Send me a picture when you have a minute. I’ll be curious to see. Does this mean the backsplash behind the stove is wood?
